title: Middleware – Concept and Built-in Middleware
description: Understand middleware and use built-in middleware like express.json().

slides.title: What is Middleware? 🔗
slides.content:
# Middleware

Middleware functions are functions that have access to the request and response objects, and the next middleware function in the application's request-response cycle.

They can:
- Execute any code.
- Modify the request and response objects.
- End the request-response cycle.
- Call the next middleware with `next()`.

If a middleware does not end the cycle, it must call `next()` to pass control to the next middleware, otherwise the request hangs.

slides.title: Writing a Simple Middleware ✍️
Built-in Middleware 🛠️
slides.content:
## Express Built-in Middleware

Express provides several built-in middleware functions:

- `express.json()` – parses incoming JSON requests and puts the parsed data in `req.body`.
- `express.urlencoded({ extended: true })` – parses URL-encoded bodies (form submissions).
- `express.static()` – serves static files.

```javascript
app.use(express.json()); // for parsing application/json
app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true })); // for forms
```

After using `express.json()`, you can access `req.body` in your route handlers.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `express.json()` middleware?

options.text: To parse JSON request bodies and populate req.body

quizData.explanation: `express.json()` parses incoming requests with JSON payloads.
